item.title: Loyal Equipments Gains 7.70%: 2 Key Factors Driving the Weekly Rebound
item.description: Loyal Equipments Ltd experienced a volatile week, ultimately gaining 7.70% from Rs.164.30 to Rs.176.95, outperforming the Sensex which declined by 0.29% over the same period. The stock rebounded strongly after hitting a 52-week low on 30 March 2026, driven by a combination of market recovery and technical repositioning amid a downgrade to a Strong Sell rating by MarketsMOJO. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: Galaxy Surfactants Gains 3.56%: 2 Key Factors Driving the Weekly Move
item.description: Galaxy Surfactants Ltd experienced a volatile week, ultimately gaining 3.56% to close at Rs.1,653.35 on 2 April 2026, outperforming the Sensex which declined by 0.29% over the same period. The stock rebounded strongly after hitting a 52-week low on 30 March, driven by a significant intraday surge on 1 April amid broader market recovery and sector strength. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: Zodiac Clothing Gains 4.99% Despite All-Time Low: 4 Key Factors Driving the Move
item.description: Zodiac Clothing Company Ltd’s stock exhibited notable volatility this week, closing with a 4.99% gain despite a turbulent start marked by a fresh 52-week and all-time low of Rs.59.2 on 30 March 2026. The stock outperformed the Sensex, which declined by 0.29% over the same period, reflecting a week of sharp reversals and heightened trading activity amid challenging sectoral and financial conditions. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: Mahanagar Telephone Nigam Ltd Gains 6.30%: 3 Key Factors Driving the Move
item.description: Mahanagar Telephone Nigam Ltd (MTNL) closed the week ending 3 April 2026 with a 6.30% gain, rising from Rs.23.01 to Rs.24.46, outperforming the Sensex which declined by 0.29% over the same period. The stock experienced significant volatility, hitting a 52-week low on 30 March before staging a strong rebound on 1 April. This review analyses the key events and market dynamics that shaped MTNL’s price action during the week. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: Bhagiradha Chemicals Gains 4.44%: 4 Key Events Shaping the Week
item.description: Bhagiradha Chemicals & Industries Ltd closed the week with a 4.44% gain, rising from Rs.189.05 on 27 March 2026 to Rs.197.45 on 2 April 2026, outperforming the Sensex which declined by 0.29% over the same period. The week was marked by significant volatility, including a sharp fall to a 52-week low followed by a strong recovery driven by a notable gap up and intraday surge. These price movements were influenced by a combination of technical factors, sectoral trends, and market sentiment shifts. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: Asian Hotels (East) Ltd Gains 6.02%: 2 Key Factors Driving the Weekly Move
item.description: Asian Hotels (East) Ltd delivered a notable weekly gain of 6.02%, closing at Rs.150.65 on 2 April 2026, outperforming the Sensex which declined by 0.29% over the same period. The stock’s upward momentum was driven by a significant technical development—the formation of a Golden Cross—and a subsequent upgrade in its investment rating from 'Strong Sell' to 'Sell' by MarketsMOJO. Despite persistent fundamental challenges, these technical improvements have shifted market sentiment, marking a cautiously optimistic phase for the micro-cap hotel and resorts company. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: JTL Industries Gains 8.56%: Key Market Moves and Technical Signals This Week
item.description: JTL Industries Ltd delivered a notable weekly gain of 8.56%, closing at Rs.49.83 on 2 April 2026, outperforming the Sensex which declined by 0.29% over the same period. The week was marked by a sharp rebound following a 52-week low, a significant gap up opening, and renewed intraday strength, reflecting a volatile but ultimately positive shift in the stock’s trajectory amid a cautious broader market environment. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: Hindusthan Urban Infrastructure Ltd Gains 16.97%: 3 Key Factors Driving the Surge
item.description: Hindusthan Urban Infrastructure Ltd delivered a strong weekly performance, rising 16.97% from Rs.467.30 to Rs.546.60 between 30 March and 2 April 2026, significantly outperforming the Sensex which declined by 0.29% over the same period. The stock’s rally was marked by a new 52-week high, technical upgrades, and volatile trading amid weak fundamental signals. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: Axis Bank Ltd. Declines 0.58%: 7 Key Factors Shaping This Week’s Volatility
item.description: Axis Bank Ltd. closed the week ending 2 April 2026 with a marginal decline of 0.58%, slightly underperforming the Sensex which fell 0.29%. The week was marked by volatile trading sessions, a notable intraday high on 1 April, and persistent bearish sentiment reflected in heavy put option activity. Despite intermittent rebounds, the stock faced pressure from broader market weakness and sectoral headwinds, culminating in a cautious outlook for investors. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: Moschip Technologies Gains 4.75%: Key Market Moves and Technical Signals This Week
item.description: Moschip Technologies Ltd closed the week ending 3 April 2026 with a notable gain of 4.75%, rising from Rs.159.00 to Rs.166.55, outperforming the Sensex which declined by 0.29% over the same period. The stock’s recovery was led by a strong gap up and intraday surge on 1 April, reversing earlier losses and signalling renewed buying interest despite prevailing bearish technical indicators and a recent downgrade in its Mojo Grade. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: Hathway Cable & Datacom Ltd Gains 5.38%: 3 Key Factors Driving the Weekly Move
item.description: Hathway Cable & Datacom Ltd’s stock rebounded strongly this week, gaining 5.38% from Rs.9.30 to Rs.9.80, outperforming the Sensex which declined by 0.29%. The week was marked by a sharp recovery following a fresh 52-week and all-time low on 30 March, with the stock surging over 9% on 1 April amid mixed market conditions. Despite this bounce, the company’s longer-term challenges remain evident in its financial and technical profile. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: Colgate-Palmolive (India) Ltd Drops 2.59%: 4 Key Factors Behind the Weekly Decline
item.description: Colgate-Palmolive (India) Ltd’s stock closed the week at Rs.1,828.90, down 2.59% from the previous Friday’s close of Rs.1,877.55, underperforming the Sensex which declined 0.29% over the same period. The week was marked by a sharp drop to a 52-week low on 30 March, followed by modest recovery attempts amid mixed technical signals and persistent market caution. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: HMT Ltd Gains 0.30%: Mixed Technical Signals and Upper Circuit Surge Define the Week
item.description: HMT Ltd recorded a modest weekly gain of 0.30%, closing at Rs.60.60 on 2 April 2026, outperforming the Sensex which declined by 0.29% over the same period. The stock experienced a volatile week marked by a sharp drop on 30 March followed by a strong rebound and an upper circuit surge on 1 April, reflecting mixed technical signals and renewed buying interest amid sector outperformance. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: Pacific Industries Ltd Gains 3.48%: 2 Key Factors Driving the Weekly Move
item.description: Pacific Industries Ltd’s stock recorded a modest weekly gain of 3.48% to close at Rs.124.95 on 2 April 2026, outperforming the Sensex which declined by 0.29% over the same period. The week was marked by a sharp rebound following a 52-week low on 30 March, driven by a significant price recovery and a notable valuation shift that tempered investor sentiment amid ongoing fundamental challenges. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: Sigma Advanced System Ltd Gains 3.20%: 3 Key Events Shaping the Week
item.description: Sigma Advanced System Ltd recorded a 3.20% gain over the week ending 2 April 2026, closing at Rs.157.95, outperforming the Sensex which declined by 0.29%. The stock experienced significant volatility, marked by a sharp lower circuit hit on 30 March, a strong upper circuit surge on 1 April, and another lower circuit event on 2 April. These swings reflect a week of intense investor activity amid a prevailing bearish technical backdrop. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: GMR Airports Ltd’s Volatile Week: -0.25% Despite Intraday Rebound and Downgrade
item.description: GMR Airports Ltd closed the week marginally lower by 0.25% at Rs.88.88, slightly outperforming the Sensex which fell 0.29%. The week was marked by a significant downgrade to a Strong Sell rating amid mixed financials and weak technicals, followed by a notable intraday surge of 5.03% on 1 April 2026, reflecting a volatile but eventful trading period for the stock. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: Tarmat Ltd Gains 4.91%: 3 Key Factors Driving the Week’s Volatility
item.description: Tarmat Ltd closed the week with a notable gain of 4.91%, rising from Rs.53.00 on 27 March 2026 to Rs.55.60 on 2 April 2026, outperforming the Sensex which declined by 0.29% over the same period. The stock experienced significant volatility, including a sharp drop early in the week followed by two consecutive days of upper circuit hits, reflecting a complex interplay of fundamental downgrades and strong speculative buying. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: Indraprastha Medical Corporation Ltd Gains 4.07%: Key Momentum Shifts and Market Dynamics
item.description: Indraprastha Medical Corporation Ltd closed the week with a 4.07% gain, rising from Rs.384.85 to Rs.400.50, outperforming the Sensex which declined by 0.29% over the same period. The stock experienced significant volatility, marked by a sharp decline on 30 March followed by a strong recovery and intraday highs on 1 and 2 April, reflecting a week of mixed technical signals and renewed buying interest amid broader market fluctuations. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: Panama Petrochem Ltd Gains 6.43%: 3 Key Factors Driving the Weekly Rebound
item.description: Panama Petrochem Ltd’s stock rebounded strongly this week, gaining 6.43% from Rs.251.00 to Rs.267.15, outperforming the Sensex which declined by 0.29%. After hitting a 52-week low on 30 March amid sustained selling pressure, the stock staged a notable recovery over the next two trading sessions, buoyed by intraday highs and improved market sentiment. Despite the rally, technical indicators remain cautious, reflecting a complex outlook for this small-cap oil sector player. Time: 3 hours ago
